title: rhamnus lycioides subsp. graeca
intro: Rhamnus lycioides subsp. graeca, also known as Greek buckthorn or Graecan rhamnus, is a plant species in the buckthorn family Rhamnaceae. Traditionally, Greek buckthorn has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its bitter and astringent compounds, which have been used to treat various health issues. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as supporting digestive health, reducing inflammation, and acting as an antioxidant. The herbal preparations of Greek buckthorn typically involve drying and processing the plant material, which can be made into teas, tinctures, or capsules for consumption.
